## [00:00 - 01:33]

This is the Public Cast Shot. I'm John Loomer and today we're going to talk about remarketing and the 80-20 rule. Let's go. Use the 80-20 rule. Dedicate 80% of your budget to prospecting and 20% to remarketing. But these things impact that approach. First, your budget. If you're only spending $10 a day, you're going to dedicate most of that budget to remarketing. That's the low-hanging fruit and the easiest way to make an impact. You can make a much bigger impact with prospecting as you spend more. Second, the size of your remarketing audience. If you have a new business with a new list and virtually no website traffic, you just can't spend much on remarketing. If you have a huge list and lots of website traffic, you have a lot more flexibility to spend more. Third, repeat customers. This is a decent portion of your remarketing. If you only sell one piece of software, most of your customers are one-time buyers. If you sell coffee beans, those customers are going to need to refill every few weeks. Fourth, is it effective? That's all that matters. How effective is your prospecting? How effective is your remarketing? Adjust those percentages to find the sweet spot. This is a start to get you going. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
